question_text: In a world where cause precedes effect is not a universal rule, consider the following events: John wakes up (A), then it starts raining (B), then John grabs his umbrella (C). What is the causal order of events?
correct_answer: A, B, C (John grabbing his umbrella caused the rain)

metadata.trap_answer: A, B, C (The rain caused John to grab his umbrella)
metadata.counterfactual_rule: Cause does not necessarily precede effect

question_text: In a reality where the grandfather paradox is resolved by changing the past instead of preventing the action, what happens if a time traveler goes back in time and kills his own grandfather before he has children?
correct_answer: The time traveler's grandfather is now a woman and has children, thus the time traveler was never born as a man but as a woman

metadata.trap_answer: The time traveler would cease to exist
metadata.counterfactual_rule: Changes to the past alter the present, but the change must be coherent and resolve the paradox

question_text: In a system where every action has an equal and opposite reaction, consider a ball thrown upwards. What happens to the ball?
correct_answer: The ball splits into two, one going up and the other going down

metadata.trap_answer: The ball goes up and then comes back down due to gravity
metadata.counterfactual_rule: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction

question_text: In a world where time is currency and can be transferred from one person to another, what happens when a person gives 5 years of their time to someone else?
correct_answer: The giver becomes 5 years younger and the receiver becomes 5 years older

metadata.trap_answer: Nothing physical happens, but the giver has less time to live and the receiver has more
metadata.counterfactual_rule: Time can be transferred from one person to another

question_text: In a reality where events repeat themselves every 5 years, what happens to a person born in 1990?
correct_answer: The person is reborn in 1995, 2000, 2005, etc., reliving the same life over and over

metadata.trap_answer: The person lives a normal life with no repeating events

question_text: In a world where causality is not transitive, consider the following events: A causes B, B causes C. What is the relationship between A and C?
In a reality where every decision creates a new universe, what happens when a person decides to wear a blue shirt instead of a red one?
correct_answer: Two universes are created, one where the person wears a blue shirt and one where they wear a red shirt

metadata.trap_answer: The person simply wears a blue shirt in the current universe
metadata.counterfactual_rule: Every decision creates a new universe

question_text: In a system where entropy always decreases over time, what happens to a cup of hot coffee left on a table?
correct_answer: The coffee becomes colder and more organized, eventually turning into a solid cube

metadata.trap_answer: The coffee becomes colder and less organized, eventually turning into a cold, messy liquid

question_text: In a world where the concept of simultaneity is absolute, what happens when two events occur at the same time in different locations?
correct_answer: The events are considered to be the same event

metadata.trap_answer: The events are considered to be separate events

question_text: In a reality where free will does not exist, what happens when a person makes a decision?
correct_answer: The person's decision is predetermined and they have no control over it

metadata.trap_answer: The person has control over their decision and can choose freely

question_text: In a system where the laws of physics change every hour, what happens to an object in motion?
correct_answer: The object's motion is affected by the changing laws of physics, potentially causing it to change direction or speed

metadata.trap_answer: The object's motion is unaffected by the changing laws of physics
metadata.counterfactual_rule: The laws of physics change every hour

question_text: In a world where time dilation is not relative, what happens to a person who travels at high speed?
correct_answer: The person experiences time dilation regardless of their relative motion to observers

metadata.trap_answer: The person experiences time dilation only relative to observers who are at rest

question_text: In a reality where the concept of 'before' and 'after' are interchangeable, what happens when a person looks at a clock?
correct_answer: The person sees the clock's hands moving in both clockwise and counterclockwise directions simultaneously

metadata.trap_answer: The person sees the clock's hands moving in only one direction
metadata.counterfactual_rule: The concepts of 'before' and 'after' are interchangeable
